name: Calibratable Trust Model Registry and Universal Model Trace Inspector
confidence_statement: The CyberShield scoring models are currently expert-derived V1 trust models. We have high confidence in the architecture because it separates trust, confidence, evidence quality, reliance risk, runtime admissibility, and defensibility. We have medium confidence in the initial scoring factors and lower confidence in the default weights until they are calibrated against test scenarios, expert review, and real-world use cases. The models are implemented as versioned, registry-backed, inspectable, and calibratable models, not hardcoded scoring truth.
controlling_principle: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
boundary: Static advisory prototype only. The registry defines expert-derived V1 calibratable models. It does not perform live scoring, live evidence retrieval, statistical validation, certification, legal determination, workflow automation, ticketing, notification, backend persistence, or enforcement.
calibration_requirements_for_90_plus_con: 20 to 50 test scenarios across internet trust, vendor trust, AI agent action, policy/control trust, and executive decisions
edge cases where trust and confidence diverge
expert review from cybersecurity, legal, audit, and AI governance perspectives
red-team attempts to manipulate the scores
score calibration after observing false positives and false negatives

assumptions: Source data has not changed since publication date
caveats: Use for briefing only after primary source verification
standard_score_object_schema.risk_if_wrong: Executive briefing error or public trust damage
standard_score_object_schema.recommended_next_step: Assign qualified reviewer and verify against primary source

trace_inspector_required_questions: What object or claim was evaluated?
What model version produced the score?
What happens if CyberShield is wrong?

core_models.purpose: Score the quality, relevance, freshness, completeness, and traceability of evidence supporting a claim, decision, control, vendor assessment, artifact, Proof Pack, or runtime action.
core_models.override_notes: Weights are defaults and may be recalibrated by scenario, industry, evidence type, and validated performance.
core_models.human_review_notes: Evidence trust should improve only when evidence is traceable, current, relevant, complete, and reviewed by an appropriate human where reliance risk is material.

core_models.purpose: Measure how certain CyberShield is about a trust score, evidence score, claim score, artifact score, or runtime decision. Do not merge confidence with trust.
core_models.override_notes: Confidence can be high even when trust is low if contradiction is clear. Confidence can be low even when a source appears trusted if evidence is incomplete.
core_models.human_review_notes: Confidence should be interpreted separately from reliance risk and runtime admissibility.

core_models.purpose: Measure how dangerous it is if a user relies on a claim, artifact, model output, vendor assertion, AI action, control status, or executive recommendation and it is wrong.
core_models.override_notes: High trust plus high reliance risk may still require caveats or approval. Moderate trust plus high reliance risk should escalate.
core_models.human_review_notes: Reliance risk asks what happens if this is wrong, not whether the evidence appears strong.

core_models.purpose: Determine whether a CyberShield action, recommendation, citation, briefing, AI agent action, vendor approval, payment change, evidence claim, or executive decision can proceed.
core_models.override_notes: Runtime admissibility is not only a number. It must produce an action band and reason for action.
core_models.human_review_notes: High-consequence, low-evidence, or contradicted actions should escalate, block, refuse, or quarantine depending on purpose and reversibility.

core_models.purpose: Determine whether a CyberShield output is ready to be shown to an executive, board, customer, regulator, auditor, attorney, investor, journalist, or public stakeholder.
core_models.override_notes: Proof Pack readiness depends on disclosure quality as much as positive evidence.
core_models.human_review_notes: Missing evidence and assumptions should improve defensibility if disclosed, not buried.

core_models.purpose: Prevent CyberShield from pretending the machine verified everything.
core_models.override_notes: Reviewer role and authority must fit the consequence and claim type.
core_models.human_review_notes: Named human review does not equal correctness. It increases traceability and accountability when review scope and limits are documented.

assumptions: Payment action has material financial consequence
caveats: Prototype does not verify banking or block payments
sample_score_outputs.risk_if_wrong: Payment may be sent to a fraudulent or unauthorized destination
sample_score_outputs.recommended_next_step: Assign controller review and attach verification evidence before approval

assumptions: AI tools may be in use without complete governance evidence
caveats: Prototype does not detect live AI usage
sample_score_outputs.risk_if_wrong: Sensitive, proprietary, regulated, or customer data may be exposed to unapproved AI systems
sample_score_outputs.recommended_next_step: Confirm approved tools, allowed data classes, and user access scope
